https://docs.wpilib.org/en/stable/: Official WPILib docs: robot project setup, command-based framework, control theory, kinematics, vision, simulation, and hardware APIs for FRC Java/C++ teams.
https://v6.docs.ctr-electronics.com/en/l: CTRE Phoenix 6 documentation for Talon FX, CANcoder, and Pigeon — configuration, control requests, Motion Magic, telemetry, and migration from Phoenix 5.
https://codedocs.revrobotics.com/java/: REV Java API reference for Spark Max, Spark Flex, and REV sensors — motor configuration, closed-loop control, encoder interfaces, and CAN bus usage.
https://pathplanner.dev/home.html: PathPlanner hub: GUI path authoring, PathPlannerLib API for holonomic and differential autos, event markers, and integration with WPILib pose estimation.
https://docs.limelightvision.io/docs/doc: Limelight 2+/3 docs: static IP setup, pipeline tuning, AprilTag and retroreflective tracking, robot pose estimation, and NetworkTables integration.
https://docs.google.com/document/d/1qKTu: Community FRC programming notes covering project structure, command-based patterns, control loops, and common subsystem implementations with worked examples.
https://docs.iowacityrobotics.org/progra: Team 167 Iowa City robotics programming guide: debugging workflow, Git usage, command-based structure, and subsystem design patterns for competition code.
https://docs.wpilib.org/en/stable/docs/s: WPILib explains command-based architecture — Subsystems, Commands, triggers, and RobotContainer — and why it separates robot logic from scheduling.
https://bovlb.github.io/frc-tips/command: Oblarg (BoVLB) command tips: default commands, requirement conflicts, parallel and sequential command groups, and common command-based pitfalls with concise examples.
https://robonauts-everybot.github.io/Eve: EveryBot code manual: starter command-based project layout, intake and elevator examples, and patterns used in low-resource EveryBot build guides.
https://v6.docs.ctr-electronics.com/en/l: Phoenix 6 control requests: DutyCycle, Velocity, Position, Motion Magic, and TorqueCurrent — how to replace legacy set() calls with explicit request objects.
CTRE Motion Magic: trapezoidal motion profiling on Talon FX with cruise velocity, acceleration, and jerk limits for smooth mechanism moves without a separate profile generator.
https://www.chiefdelphi.com/t/is-this-re: Chief Delphi PID tuning thread: practical kP/kI/kD workflow on real mechanisms, oscillation diagnosis, and when feedforward matters more than integral gain.
https://docs.google.com/document/d/13aGn: Hands-on PID simulation exercises using WPILib physics sim — tune elevator and arm gains with recorded plots before moving to hardware.
https://docs.limelightvision.io/docs/doc: LimelightHelpers / LimelightLib: Java API for pose estimates, target alignment, and pipeline data over NetworkTables without writing raw HTTP or JSON parsers.
Limelight AprilTag pipelines: family selection, multi-tag pose solve, field layout JSON, and tuning exposure for reliable FRC field localization.
https://www.chiefdelphi.com/t/from-zero-: Step-by-step reef auto-align using Limelight target offsets, rotation-first alignment, and PID drive commands — includes code structure and tuning notes.
https://youtu.be/rKTio2rlF_o: Video walkthrough: Limelight pipeline setup, reading tx/ty/ ta in code, and driving rotation-to-target for vision-assisted teleop or auto.
https://pathplanner.dev/gui-getting-star: PathPlanner GUI tutorial: draw paths and constraints, set ideal starting pose, export to deploy folder, and wire PathPlannerAuto in RobotContainer.
https://docs.advantagekit.org/getting-st: AdvantageKit Talon FX swerve template: pre-wired odometry, module optimization, SysId hooks, and log replay for CTRE swerve drivetrains.
https://youtu.be/vPrRsnVQgf4: Video intro to FRC autonomous: timed sequences vs sensor-based moves, chooser setup, and when to add path following to a baseline auto.
https://yagsl.gitbook.io/yams/documentat: YAMS (Yet Another Motor Simulator): WPILib mechanism sim for elevators, arms, and intakes — plant tuning, visualization, and validating gains before deploy.
YAMS exponential motion profiles: configure max velocity/acceleration and generate smooth setpoints for mechanisms that need curved velocity ramps.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Vr-79QyL: State machine concepts for FRC: states, transitions, entry/exit actions, and replacing nested if-chains in teleop and auto with explicit state charts.
https://docs.wpilib.org/en/stable/docs/s: WPILib Git intro: clone WPILib projects, commit workflow, .gitignore for build artifacts, and collaborating without breaking Gradle vendor deps.
https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1: Slide deck on FRC Git workflow: branches, remotes, merge conflicts in robot projects, and coordinating code changes across subteams.
https://youtu.be/L_Ism2q3m6c: Pull request walkthrough: fork/branch workflow, opening a PR on GitHub, review comments, and merging safely into a shared robot repository.
https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PL: Video series on FRC Java programming: command-based structure, subsystem examples, control tuning, and competition code organization.
https://deep-blue-training.readthedocs.i: Deep Blue (Team 384) training docs: swerve odometry, auto routines, command-based conventions, and advanced control topics used in their open-source projects.
https://www.spectrum3847.org/: Spectrum 3847 team site: technical blog posts, CAD/software release notes, and links to their training materials and open repositories.
https://www.thecompassalliance.org/: Compass Alliance curriculum: staged FRC learning paths from mechanical basics through programming, scouting, and team management resources.
https://developer.android.com/studio: Download Android Studio IDE, SDK Manager, and official guides for installing the Android SDK used to build and deploy FTC Robot Controller apps.
https://ftc-docs.firstinspires.org/en/la: FIRST FTC Android Studio tutorial: install SDK, clone FtcRobotController, open TeamCode, deploy OpModes, and connect to Control Hub over ADB/Wi‑Fi.
FTC OpMode reference: @TeleOp vs @Autonomous, linear vs iterative lifecycle, hardware map access, and gamepad/telemetry patterns in the SDK.
https://github.com/FIRST-Tech-Challenge/: Official FTC SDK repository: FtcRobotController module (do not edit) and TeamCode template for season-specific OpModes, Gradle build, and OnBot Java compatibility.
/ftc/control-hub-expansion-hub: Mantik lesson on the REV Control Hub and Expansion Hub: USB/device naming, motor and servo ports, I2C sensors, configuration on Driver Hub, and wiring checks before running OpModes.
/ftc/telemetry-logging: Mantik lesson on FTC telemetry: addLine, addData, update(), formatting driver feedback on the Driver Station, and debugging sensor values during teleop and auto.
